title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Stock Drops 8% on Double Downgrade

title: S&P sinks under weight of bearish shift for Norwegian Cruise Lines
text: 12:05pm: Dow, Nasdaq down on hawkish Fed sentiment The major US indices continued to fall midday, as Federal Reserve presidents signaled they're not planning to stop interest rate hikes. At midday, the S&P 500 was down by 0.5% at 3,936, the Nasdaq Composite was down by 0.4% at 11,136, while the Dow Jones was down by 0.07% to 33,530 points.

title: Norwegian Cruise stock is S&P 500's biggest loser after Credit Suisse swings to bearish
text: Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH, -8.27% slumped 7.8% in morning trading Thursday, to make them the S&P 500's SPX, -1.17% biggest losers on the day, after Credit Suisse swung to bearish from bullish on the cruise operator, citing valuation concerns relative to its peers.

title: Global Ports Holding's results point to continued recovery in cruise industry
text: Global Ports Holding's (GPH) first half trading update demonstrated the continued recovery in the cruise industry with a four-fold increase in revenue and a swing back into profit taking Cruise EBITDA back to pre-Covid levels. The company, the world's largest cruise port operator, reported a leap in adjusted revenue to US$64mln from US$14.8mln while adjusted EBITDA of US$40.4mln compared to a loss of US$0.5mln this time last year.

title: Norwegian Cruise stock jumps toward 6-month high after results beat expectation, key earnings metric turns positive
text: Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH, -1.59% jumped 2.4% toward a six-month high in premarket trading Tuesday, after the cruise operator reported a narrower-than-expected third-quarter loss on revenue that rose above forecasts, and as an adjusted earnings metric reached profitability for the first time since the start of the pandemic.
